mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next month when the policy rate trend is down. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because easing without renewed inflation pressure supports multiple expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are negative in a majority of periods where FEDFUNDS is falling over a 3-month window, or if the signal loses positive rank association in monetary easing windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent baseline signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress equity earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is more reliable when PAYEMS is also decelerating, because broad labor-market deterioration confirms the demand slowdown.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points versus 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of UNRATE-rise triggers despite PAYEMS slowing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST indicates weakening residential demand and lower economy-wide growth momentum, which should lead the S&P 500 lower over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when housing weakness is offset by broad retail sales strength, because then the housing signal is not capturing economy-wide demand loss.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns remain positive during sustained HOUST downtrends, or if the predictive sign flips to positive when RSAFS is flat or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A positive gap between INDPRO growth and PAYEMS growth implies labor productivity gains and improving unit cost conditions, which supports corporate margins and equity returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when production accelerates while employment growth remains weak, because output gains without labor overheating are margin-accretive.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO-minus-PAYEMS episodes do not outperform on 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears when both series accelerate together.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates above recent levels, higher inflation raises discount rates and policy-tightening risk, which should push the S&P 500 lower over the next 20 trading days. This mechanism is activated only when FEDFUNDS is already elevated, because inflation surprises matter more when policy is constrained by prior tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rate rises above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or if the sign is not negative in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rise in RSAFS signals stronger nominal consumer spending and better revenue growth for large-cap firms, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect should be absent when UNRATE is rising, because labor deterioration would offset the sales momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive on average during RSAFS uptrends, or if the signal remains positive even when UNRATE is rising.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE marks a genuine labor-market improvement, which should lift risk appetite and push the S&P 500 higher over the next quarter. This is a true interaction because payroll gains alone are weaker when unemployment is not falling, and unemployment declines alone are weaker without payroll growth.
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE do not deliver above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the sign as well as the pair.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO with stable or falling CPIAUCSL indicates supply-side expansion without inflation pressure, supporting margins and lower recession risk, so the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism fails during demand shocks, when higher production reflects inventory rebuilds rather than durable activity.
failure_condition: Reject if industrial upswings with flat or falling CPIAUCSL do not produce positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect is negative in periods of inventory-led production gains.
